contexts.context: .... These lines afford more than one example of <mark>climax</mark>, graceful repetition, and richness of poetic language...
...is made subservient to utility; but the beautiful <mark>climax</mark> is destroyed by which the inferior kinds are connected...
...Shylock and his subsequent conversion to Christianity form the <mark>climax</mark> of the play's plot. [LD]  Siddons...
